brief_justification: Correctly identifies the path as viable with valid hop-by-hop rule verification; explores multiple levels including packet transit semantics, pivot mechanics, and second-order trust-chain implications; shows awareness of interpretive ambiguity by splitting direct-transit from pivot scenarios, though does not explicitly name cognitive biases; creates an explicit rule-by-rule table verifying every hop independently; acknowledges that viability depends on attack type (direct vs. pivot) but does not state confidence levels or enumerate what assumptions would invalidate the conclusion; systematically evaluates two distinct attack interpretations and explains exactly why one is blocked while the other is viable; full derivation table plus step-by-step pivot narrative allows complete reproduction of reasoning by a third party
